name: Reibekuchen (German Potatoe Pancake)
recipeIngredient: 1.5 kg potatoes (a variety that stays firm when cooked - not the type used for mashed potatoes)
a small amount of flour (Buchweizen, wheat flour or oat flakes)
recipeInstructions:
1. Grate the potatoes through a sieve to allow water to drain.
Do not use a very fine grater; you do not want mashed potatoes.
2. Squeeze out excess water from the grated potatoes.
3. Grate the onion finely and mix it with the grated potatoes, eggs, salt, and flour (or oat flakes) until combined.
4. Heat oil in a pan. Using a spatula, scoop some batter into the hot oil to form thin cakes.
5. Cook until golden on both sides, turning occasionally.
The edges should be crispy when cooked through.
6. Serve hot, often with butter and optional toppings such as Rübenkraut (sugar-root syrup) or Apfelkraut (apple syrup).
